fictional_company_description: A regional direct-to-consumer auto and renters insurer operating across 14 states with approximately 290,000 active policies and $380M in written premium, growing its agency channel through a new digital quoting platform.
analyst_frame: As the data analyst II at Waypoint Property & Casualty, I have been asked to analyze policyholder retention behavior and household lifetime value to identify at-risk renewal segments and quantify the revenue impact of the multi-product attachment and digital channel acquisition gaps.
role_target: Data Analyst II -- Direct Agency Advancement
archetype_rationale: Q01 and Q07 are time-series and cohort-based (Archetype B); Q02 and Q05 require binary classification with SHAP (Archetype A); Q04 and Q10 are financial and funnel-diagnostic. A pure archetype misses the retention lifecycle story.

approved_business_questions.question: What do first-year renewal rates look like across policy cohorts by acquisition quarter, and which cohorts show the steepest attrition before the 12-month mark?
Which policyholder characteristics are most predictive of non-renewal at 12 months, and how early in the policy term can a lapse be predicted?
approved_business_questions.primary_skill: Python (gradient boosting, SHAP), SQL (behavioral feature engineering)

approved_business_questions.question: How does policyholder lifetime value vary by acquisition channel and coverage tier, and which segments generate the most revenue per policy over a 36-month window?
Which billing and payment behaviors correlate most strongly with early lapse, and can a lapse warning score be built from them?
Do policyholders acquired through the digital quoting channel show different first-year retention curves than those acquired through traditional agency channels?
What is the relationship between claims history and renewal behavior, and does it differ by coverage tier and acquisition channel?
What is the auto-to-renters cross-sell conversion rate by agency type and customer tenure band, and where does the cross-sell funnel drop off?

dataset.target_definition: 1 = policy renewed at 12 months, 0 = lapsed or cancelled
dataset.renewal_window_note: Target applies to policies effective on or before 2024-12-31 (~72,000 policies with known renewal outcome)

source_tables.name: fact_billing_summary
source_tables.grain: One row per policy (billing behavior summary over term)

leakage_columns.reason: Derived from full 36-month policy outcome. Not available at renewal time.
Only known after renewal decision is made.
Only known after renewal decision is made.
Legitimate feature ONLY if used with strict date guard ensuring contact occurred before renewal decision.

dashboard.title: Waypoint Property and Casualty | Retention Analytics
dashboard.subtitle: Auto and Renters Policyholder Renewal | 2023-2025 | 90,000 Policies

tabs.name: Retention Overview
tabs.layout: Full-width cohort heatmap, paired cohort line + severity bar, full-width dual-axis
charts: Renewal Rate by Claims Severity Band (bar)
Renewal Rate vs. Claim Payout Amount (dual-axis)

tabs.name: Channel and Value
tabs.layout: Paired bars, full-width dual-axis trend, paired bars
charts: AQX vs. Non-AQX Renewal Rate Over Time (dual-axis line)
Renewal Rate by Agency Type and Tenure Band (grouped bar)

tabs.name: Predictive Model
tabs.layout: Paired lift + gain, paired SHAP + confusion matrix, paired CSS histogram + tiles
charts: At-Risk Policyholder Profile Explorer (metric tiles + radio)

tabs.name: Cross-Sell Funnel
tabs.layout: Full-width CSS funnel + narrative, paired conversion bars
charts: Renters Cross-Sell Rate by Agency Type (bar)
Renters Cross-Sell Rate by Customer Tenure Band (bar)

tabs.name: Financial Impact
tabs.layout: Two-slider simulator + results card + scenario bar, reactive paired charts below
charts: Renewal Rate Simulator (two sliders + results card)
Additional Renewing Policies by Channel (reactive bar)
Current vs. Projected CLTV by Coverage Tier (reactive stacked bar)

tabs.name: Recommendations
tabs.layout: Context paragraph, three tiered sections, two cards per tier

tabs.name: Healthcare Application
tabs.layout: Two-column: translation table left, lever cards right, portability strip below

github_settings.description: Analyzes 90,000 synthetic insurance policies to predict first-year lapse risk, identify a $7.8M cross-sell gap, and map the auto-to-renters funnel across agency segments, achieving 1.59x lift in the top lapse-risk decile with XGBoost and SHAP.
github_settings.suggested_description: Analyzes 90,000 synthetic insurance policies to predict first-year lapse risk, identify a $7.8M cross-sell gap, and map the auto-to-renters funnel across agency segments, achieving 1.59x lift in the top lapse-risk decile with XGBoost and SHAP.
